item.title: K-pop, markets edition
item.description: On Kospi index concentration

item.title: Labour and the Tories need to outflank the extremists
item.description: Kemi Badenoch and Andy Burnham must remember that elections are not fought and won on issues of culture alone

item.title: How to stop AI becoming the enemy of younger workers
item.description: ‘Seniority-biased’ hiring patterns in South Korea carry a lesson for the rest of the world

item.title: Should you put a bath in the bedroom?
item.description: Inspired by boutique hotels, the hybrid trend is growing. Faff, say some. Fabulous, say others

item.title: Volkswagen needs sense-makers at the top
item.description: Carmaker will have to cultivate capacity to improvise as it navigates the profound uncertainty it faces

item.title: How to manage the next emerging markets capital flows boom
item.description: Foreign exchange reserves are not the only way to limit the build-up of speculative, short-term liabilities

item.title: EasyJet’s sale could be surprisingly good for its rivals
item.description: Budget airline will comfort its competitors if the destination remains largely the same

item.title: The next frontier for defence companies is the deep blue sea
item.description: Countries have a whole host of relatively new vulnerabilities lying on the seabed

item.title: The UK could help end Sudan’s civil war
item.description: The British government has leverage and should use it to proscribe a terrorist militia and cut off its supply lines

item.title: The flaws in Trump’s Venezuela strategy
item.description: The US-backed socialist regime has botched its response to the earthquakes

item.title: Burnham must avoid Starmer’s Trump mistake
item.description: Experience shows that voters reward leaders who stand up to the US president

item.title: Welcome to the age of the Profit Dollar
item.description: The greenback is no longer a reserve currency — it has become a vehicle for unfettered capital accumulation

item.title: Putin is running out of options
item.description: The Russian leader may want to escalate his war — but that is harder than it sounds

item.title: Trump’s drug price crusade puts Europe on guard
item.description: UK has given in to pharma industry lobbying but some EU member states promise to stand firm

item.title: Lionel Messi, Serena Williams and the obsession with Goats
item.description: Stars are using their long careers to transcend what happens on the court or the pitch

item.title: SaaSpocalypse deferred
item.description: The moats hold, for now

item.title: Why OpenAI and Anthropic may struggle to float
item.description: The costs of remaining at the frontier of AI are punishing, but the penalties for falling behind may be even worse

item.title: Andy Burnham needs to start by understanding how little he knows
item.description: Even if he could do all he wants, it would be unlikely to solve the UK’s growth problem

item.title: US regulator’s change of tack comes with risk of obscuring audit flaws
item.description: The PCAOB has signalled a shift from individual checks towards firm-wide quality controls

item.title: Investors must be wary of the earnings bubble
item.description: The real difference between today’s market boom and the late 1990s lies in capital expenditure

item.title: The reality check of leaving the abstract world of finance
item.description: Why banking does not prepare you for corporate life

item.title: Data centres are a crucial test of US industrial resolve
item.description: America must not repeat the mistakes that handed China rare-earth dominance

item.title: Saving Nato in the era of Trump
item.description: The big questions hanging over the alliance will not be discussed in Ankara

item.title: The Europeanisation of Nato is ever more important
item.description: Transatlantic military alliance will be key to the continent’s future security

item.title: Rich nations must unleash land, labour, energy and capital
item.description: As policymakers shift towards industrial interventionism, they risk neglecting the simplest drivers of growth
